author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--ci-cd-restructure
content:
Result: Restructured CI/CD into 4 workflow files for git flow (GH #129):

1. ci.yml — Full validation suite (lint, security, test 2 OS, proptest, fuzz)
   Triggers: push develop, PR develop, workflow_call
   Removed: build-release job, main branch triggers
   Added: concurrency groups

2. ci-feature.yml (NEW) — Fast feature branch CI
   Triggers: push feature/**
   Jobs: build + unit tests + integration tests (ubuntu only, <2 min target)

3. publish.yml — Release pipeline
   Triggers: tag v*, PR main
   Jobs: ci (via workflow_call) + dry-run (on PR) or publish (on tag)
   Removed: paths filter (unnecessary in git flow)
   Added: explanatory comments

4. release-builds.yml (NEW) — Binary artifacts
   Triggers: tag v*, PR main
   Jobs: build release binaries (linux, macos, windows)

5. docs.yml — Unchanged

Key improvements:
- Eliminated duplicate CI on tag pushes (was: ci.yml + publish.yml->ci.yml = 2x)
- Feature branches get fast feedback (~80% less work than full suite)
- PRs to develop skip expensive 3-platform release builds (~30% savings)
- Concurrency groups cancel stale runs on rapid pushes
- Clear workflow boundaries: each file has a single responsibility
